title: Fix journald logs
body:
#9249

This PR contains a bunch of fixes to get logs testing on journald. I have a version of common that uses journald as default vendored in, I'll remove it if/when tests pass

This also needs to be updated later to have test coverage for k8s files once we switch over to default journald (otherwise k8s files wouldn't be tested at all)

comments.body:
LGTM
Can we get the entrie logs_test to run on journal as well as k8s_file.  Is there a way to run through these tests twice, once for each type of log driver?

@rhatdan I'm not sure, I haven't played with ginkgo enough to know, but I can take a look and poke around with it. Worst comes to worst we just copy the file and add `--log-driver=k8s-file` in every test.

@ashley-cui I am thinking of something like:
[logs_test.patch.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/5999475/logs_test.patch.txt)
Basically I wrap all of the logs_test.go that are not specific to journald and run each with all supported log-drivers in a loop.
@rhatdan thanks! I'll take a look, that might be the solution, just a simple for loop :)
